items.title: Auto-Detect Installed Harnesses
items.description: Scan the system for installed AI coding CLIs (Codex, Gemini, Aider, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) and automatically suggest adding them as sync targets. Users often don't know which harnesses HarnessSync supports, so they miss easy wins. This removes the need to manually configure targets and surfaces opportunities the user didn't know existed.

items.title: Harness Capability Gap Report
items.description: Generate a report showing which Claude Code features have no equivalent in each target harness and what fidelity loss users should expect. Users configure features in Claude Code without knowing they'll silently not apply in Codex or Aider. This transforms silent failures into visible tradeoffs, helping users make informed harness choices.

items.title: Manual Edit Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness config has been manually edited since the last sync, show a structured diff and let the user choose to overwrite, merge, or skip. Currently, sync silently overwrites manual edits. This is a common pain point for teams where one member tweaks Gemini config directly — their work disappears on the next sync.

items.title: Branch-Aware Sync
items.description: Automatically re-sync when switching git branches, since different branches may have different CLAUDE.md rules or MCP configs. Developers working across feature branches and main often get confused when their AI tool behavior doesn't match the branch they just checked out. Branch-aware sync makes the AI behave consistently with the current codebase context.

items.title: Team Sync Config Sharing via Git
items.description: Commit a `.harness-sync.json` team config to the repo so all teammates automatically get the same multi-harness setup when they join the project. Teams waste hours each onboarding a new dev who has to manually configure Cursor rules, Aider conventions, and Codex agents from scratch. This makes the AI setup a first-class artifact of the repo.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Translation
items.description: Use Claude to translate CLAUDE.md rules that have no direct 1:1 mapping in target formats, generating semantically equivalent instructions in the target's native idiom. Current adapters do structural translation but lose intent when formats differ fundamentally. An AI layer can bridge the semantic gap — e.g., translating a Claude Code permission model into an Aider convention comment.

items.title: Sync History Timeline with Diff Viewer
items.description: A visual timeline of all past syncs with the ability to see exactly what changed in each sync and restore any previous state. Users who notice broken behavior in a target harness have no visibility into what changed or when. A timeline with per-file diffs makes debugging sync regressions fast and obvious.

items.title: Privacy Filter for Sensitive Config
items.description: Automatically strip or redact API keys, absolute paths, internal hostnames, and other sensitive data before syncing to targets. This is critical for teams using shared repos or syncing to cloud-connected harnesses. Without it, users risk accidentally leaking credentials embedded in MCP server configs or rule files.

items.title: One-Click New Harness Setup
items.description: A guided `/sync-onboard <harness>` workflow that installs the harness CLI if needed, configures it from your current Claude Code setup, and validates the result — all in one command. Setting up a new harness from scratch is friction-heavy. This turns 'add Aider to my workflow' from an hour-long task into a one-liner.

items.title: Skill Sync Tags (all/subset/exclude)
items.description: Add a YAML frontmatter tag to skills to control which harnesses they sync to: `sync: [codex, gemini]` or `sync: exclude-aider`. Users have skills that only make sense in Claude Code's rich environment and shouldn't pollute simpler harnesses. Tagging gives precise control without managing multiple skill directories.

items.title: Harness Usage Staleness Alerts
items.description: Detect when a sync target harness hasn't been used in 30+ days and prompt the user to remove it from the sync list. Users add harnesses they end up never using, which wastes sync time and adds maintenance burden. Staleness detection keeps the sync list lean and meaningful.

items.title: Sync Pause / Lockdown Mode
items.description: A `/sync-pause` command that temporarily suspends all auto-sync hooks, with an optional timer to auto-resume. Developers debugging config issues often need to make temporary changes to a target harness without HarnessSync immediately overwriting them. A pause mode gives them a safe window to experiment.

items.title: Multi-Repo Project Sync
items.description: Sync global Claude Code settings to multiple local repos at once — useful for monorepo setups or developers who switch between many projects. Users who work on 10+ repos must manually trigger sync in each. A global sync command propagates changes everywhere without requiring the user to cd into each repo.

items.title: Shareable Sync Templates / Presets
items.description: Pre-built sync configurations for common setups: 'frontend team', 'ML research', 'open source maintainer'. Users don't know what a good multi-harness setup looks like. Templates lower the barrier to getting started and encode community best practices for which harnesses to sync what.

items.title: Harness Version Change Tracker
items.description: Monitor installed harness versions and alert when a harness update might break existing sync mappings. When Gemini CLI changes its config format, synced files silently become invalid. Version tracking lets HarnessSync proactively warn users before they discover broken behavior in production.

items.title: Portable Config Bundle Export
items.description: Export your entire multi-harness setup as a self-contained archive (zip or tarball) that can be imported on a new machine or shared with a colleague. New machine setup is painful — users have to re-configure every harness from scratch. A bundle export/import makes setup a one-command operation.

items.title: GitHub Actions Sync Workflow
items.description: A generated GitHub Actions workflow that syncs harness configs on PR merge to main, ensuring the team repo always has up-to-date adapter configs committed. Teams who store harness configs in git currently rely on individuals remembering to sync. CI-driven sync makes it automatic and auditable via git history.

items.title: MCP Server Discovery & Sync
items.description: Browse a curated list of popular MCP servers, add them to your Claude Code config, and automatically propagate them to all target harnesses that support MCP. Users don't know what MCP servers exist and manually copying server configs across harnesses is error-prone. A discovery + sync flow makes MCP a shared resource.

items.title: Rule Inheritance: Global + Project Overrides
items.description: Support a two-tier rule system: global rules in `~/.claude/` that apply everywhere, and project-level overrides in the project's `.claude/` that extend or replace them. Users configure the same base rules repeatedly across projects. Inheritance eliminates duplication and makes updating a global rule propagate instantly everywhere.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Consistency Checker
items.description: Run a test prompt against multiple harnesses and compare how each interprets the synced rules — highlighting behavioral divergence. Users assume synced configs mean identical behavior but harnesses interpret rules differently. Behavioral divergence reports surface where the translation fidelity is lowest.

items.title: Desktop Notifications for Sync Events
items.description: Send macOS/Linux desktop notifications when auto-sync triggers: what changed, which harnesses were updated, and if any errors occurred. Users who work with auto-sync enabled have no awareness of when syncs happen or if they fail. Push notifications create ambient awareness without requiring manual status checks.

items.title: Config Dependency Visualization
items.description: Generate a visual graph showing how skills, agents, MCP servers, and rules relate to each other and which targets they sync to. Large setups become opaque — users don't know which skills depend on which MCP servers or which configs are harness-exclusive. A dependency graph makes the entire setup legible at a glance.

items.title: Sync Lint with Auto-Fix Suggestions
items.description: Extend `/sync-lint` to not just report issues but offer one-click auto-fixes: normalize formats, remove deprecated keys, fill missing required fields. Lint errors are useful but fixing them manually is tedious. Auto-fix turns lint from a warning tool into an active maintenance assistant.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, generate a human-readable changelog entry describing what config changed and why — derived from git commit messages and file diffs. Teams using HarnessSync in repos have no audit trail of why configs changed. A changelog ties sync events to the git history that triggered them.

items.title: Parity Score Dashboard
items.description: A dashboard showing a 0-100% parity score for each target harness — how much of your Claude Code setup is successfully replicated vs. lost in translation. Users have no quantitative sense of how well their setup is preserved across harnesses. A parity score gives an at-a-glance health metric and motivates closing gaps.

items.title: Conditional Sync Rules Engine
items.description: Define sync conditions: 'only sync MCP servers to Cursor if they are reachable', 'skip Aider sync on CI', 'exclude debug rules from production branches'. Static sync is too rigid for complex workflows. Conditional rules let power users express environment-aware sync logic without writing custom scripts.

items.title: Skill Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Generate a table showing which of your skills work natively in each target harness, which degrade gracefully, and which are incompatible. Users write skills for Claude Code and hope they work elsewhere. A compatibility matrix surfaces incompatibilities before sync, preventing silent no-ops in other harnesses.

items.title: Reverse Sync: Import from Another Harness
items.description: Pull rules or conventions from a target harness (Aider CONVENTIONS.md, Cursor .mdc rules) back into Claude Code as a one-time import. Teams that started with Cursor or Aider have invested rules they don't want to rewrite. Reverse sync lets them graduate to Claude Code as source of truth without losing prior work.

items.title: Config Health Score with Trend
items.description: Track the overall health of each target's synced config over time, showing whether health is improving or degrading as the Claude Code config evolves. A single-snapshot health report is useful, but users want to know if things are getting better or worse over time. Trending health catches slow-moving config rot.

items.title: Sync Webhook Notifications
items.description: Fire a webhook to a URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint) after each sync completes, with a summary of changes. Teams want Slack notifications when the shared AI config changes. Webhooks enable HarnessSync to plug into existing team notification infrastructure with zero extra tooling.

items.title: Harness Usage Profiler
items.description: Track which harness the user invokes most frequently per project and surface that data in /sync-status, suggesting the optimal sync priority ordering. Users waste time syncing rarely-used harnesses first. Usage profiling lets HarnessSync optimize sync order and prune dead targets automatically.

items.title: MCP Config Pre-Sync Validator
items.description: Before syncing MCP server configs to a target, validate that each server is reachable and returns the expected tool manifest — blocking bad configs from propagating. Users who sync unreachable MCP servers break all harnesses at once. Pre-sync validation creates a safety gate so only working servers get distributed.

items.title: IDE Extension Config Sync
items.description: Extend sync to IDE-level AI extensions: VS Code Copilot instructions, JetBrains AI Assistant prompts, Zed AI config. Power users have AI assistants in their IDE that aren't CLI harnesses. Syncing to IDE extensions brings Claude Code's rules into every coding surface, not just the terminal.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
